CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Continued To Nov-10-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Extension Of Time For Filings And Responses Due To Conflicts With San Mateo Court Related Cases
Off Calendar Jul-29-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RHONDA WOO, Esq. of Howard Rome Martin & Ridley LLP (Tel: 650-365-7715) for defendant City of San Mateo;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS LEONARD HASKIN AND P. LEIA LUCAS' Motion For Extension Of Time For Filings And Responses Due To Conflicts With San Mateo Court Related Cases.

The Court notes it received an email from plaintiff Leonard Haskin in which Leonard Haskin states the Court did not timely post a tentative ruling for this matter. The Court notes that it has verified a tentative ruling was timely posted on the court's website for this matter yesterday by 1 p.m. The Court notes that by reply email to Mr. Haskin, the Court invited Mr. Haskin to appear at today's hearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court notes that Mr. Haskin is not present today either in person or remotely via Zoom.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion for an extension of time is denied. The Court previously continued these motions for over a month and plaintiff does not persuasively demonstrate that a further continuance is necessary.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RHONDA WOO, Esq. of Howard Rome Martin & Ridley LLP (Tel: 650-365-7715) for defendant City of San Mateo;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The Court notes it received an email from plaintiff Leonard Haskin in which Leonard Haskin states the Court did not timely post a tentative ruling for this matter. The Court notes that it has verified a tentative ruling was timely posted on the court's website for this matter yesterday by 1 p.m. The Court notes that by reply email to Mr. Haskin, the Court invited Mr. Haskin to appear at today's hearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court notes that Mr. Haskin is not present today either in person or remotely via Zoom.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The matter is off calendar as moot.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

City Of San Mateo Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Jul-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RHONDA WOO, Esq. of Howard Rome Martin & Ridley LLP (Tel: 650-365-7715) for defendant City of San Mateo;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN MATEO's Motion To Transfer Venue.

The Court notes it received an email from plaintiff Leonard Haskin in which Leonard Haskin states the Court did not timely post a tentative ruling for this matter. The Court notes that it has verified a tentative ruling was timely posted on the court's website for this matter yesterday by 1 p.m. The Court notes that by reply email to Mr. Haskin, the Court invited Mr. Haskin to appear at today's hearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court notes that Mr. Haskin is not present today either in person or remotely via Zoom.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City of San Mateo's ("City") motion to change venue to San Mateo County is granted.

City has a mandatory right to have this action heard in San Mateo County. Code of Civil Procedure section 394 provides in pertinent part: "[A]ny action or proceeding against the city, county, . . . for injury occurring within the city, county . . . to person . . . caused by the negligence or alleged negligence of such city, county . . . or its agents or employees, shall be tried in such county . . . ."

"The statute unequivocally provides that an action against a county [or City] for personal injuries occurring in that county, shall be brought in defendant county." (County of Orange v. Superior Court (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 1189, 1192.) It is an exception to the general venue statute that permits an action to proceed at the defendant's residence or where the injury occurred. (Id. at 1191; see also Ventura Unified School District v Superior Court (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 811, 815 [section 394 confers on a public entity "the absolute right to change of venue to the county where the accident occurred."]; Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 743 ["in actions against local agencies, based on negligent injury to a person or property by a local agency venue is proper in the county in which the agency resides if the injury occurred there" and an "action must be transferred to the local agency's county if the requirements of section 394 are met, even if venue is properly situated where another defendant resides."].)

If the San Mateo Bench is recused from hearing plaintiff's action, then the San Mateo Superior Court and the Judicial Council will facilitate the appointment of a judge to hear this case.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
